description:
You are a customer-support engineer gathering diagnostics from a malfunctioning application.  
Inside the working directory “/home/user/support/diagnostics” you will find an existing CSV file named “logs.csv”.  
The goal is to isolate the problematic entries, convert them to JSON, and record a quick head-count summary.

WHAT YOU MUST DO  
1. Read “/home/user/support/diagnostics/logs.csv”.  
   • The CSV has the header row:  
     timestamp,service,status,message  
   • Retain the exact header spelling and order whenever you copy rows elsewhere.

2. Extract every row whose “status” column is the literal string “ERROR” (case-sensitive).  
   • Write those rows, including the header, into a new file called  
     /home/user/support/diagnostics/errors.csv

3. Convert that errors.csv file into JSON.  
   • The resulting file must be written as  
     /home/user/support/diagnostics/errors.json  
   • Required JSON formatting:  
     ‑ The file must be a single JSON array.  
     ‑ Each array element is a JSON object containing four string keys in this order:  
       timestamp, service, status, message  
     ‑ Pretty-print the output with an indentation of exactly 4 spaces.  
     Example of two objects (spacing matters):  
     [  
         {  
             "timestamp": "2023-06-12T10:16:12Z",  
             "service": "db",  
             "status": "ERROR",  
             "message": "Connection timeout"  
         },  
         {  
             "timestamp": "2023-06-12T10:17:43Z",  
             "service": "auth",  
             "status": "ERROR",  
             "message": "Invalid credentials"  
         }  
     ]

4. Count how many objects (i.e., ERROR rows) are in errors.json and write the number to  
   /home/user/support/diagnostics/diagnostics_summary.log  
   • The log file must contain exactly one line with the following format, followed by a newline:  
     Total ERROR events: X  
     Replace X with the integer count you obtained.

VERIFICATION NOTES  
Automated tests will check for the presence and byte-exact contents of three new files:

A) /home/user/support/diagnostics/errors.csv  
B) /home/user/support/diagnostics/errors.json  
C) /home/user/support/diagnostics/diagnostics_summary.log  

Pay special attention to:  
• Case sensitivity (“ERROR” only).  
• The indentation (4 spaces) in errors.json.  
• No extra or missing whitespace in diagnostics_summary.log.  

You should only need a small set of typical command-line tools (e.g., grep, awk, jq, python – whichever you prefer).  
No root access is required.
truth:
Initial file that already exists before the agent starts:

/home/user/support/diagnostics/logs.csv  
(timestamp,service,status,message CSV; 8 data rows)

Exact contents (including trailing newline at end of file):
timestamp,service,status,message
2023-06-12T10:15:00Z,auth,OK,User login successful
2023-06-12T10:16:12Z,db,ERROR,Connection timeout
2023-06-12T10:17:43Z,auth,ERROR,Invalid credentials
2023-06-12T10:18:07Z,web,OK,Page served
2023-06-12T10:19:22Z,cache,OK,Cache refreshed
2023-06-12T10:20:55Z,db,ERROR,Deadlock detected
2023-06-12T10:21:30Z,web,OK,Page served
2023-06-12T10:22:18Z,auth,OK,Password reset requested

Files that must exist when the agent is done:

1) /home/user/support/diagnostics/errors.csv  
Exact contents (header plus the three ERROR rows, newline after each row, including the last one):
timestamp,service,status,message
2023-06-12T10:16:12Z,db,ERROR,Connection timeout
2023-06-12T10:17:43Z,auth,ERROR,Invalid credentials
2023-06-12T10:20:55Z,db,ERROR,Deadlock detected

2) /home/user/support/diagnostics/errors.json  
Exact contents (note 4-space indentation, newline after each line, final ‘]’ followed by newline):
[
    {
        "timestamp": "2023-06-12T10:16:12Z",
        "service": "db",
        "status": "ERROR",
        "message": "Connection timeout"
    },
    {
        "timestamp": "2023-06-12T10:17:43Z",
        "service": "auth",
        "status": "ERROR",
        "message": "Invalid credentials"
    },
    {
        "timestamp": "2023-06-12T10:20:55Z",
        "service": "db",
        "status": "ERROR",
        "message": "Deadlock detected"
    }
]

3) /home/user/support/diagnostics/diagnostics_summary.log  
Exact contents (single line, trailing newline):
Total ERROR events: 3

Permissions: all files are user-writable (default umask 022).  
No other files are needed for verification.
